result:
The fix is complete. Here's a summary:

## Root Cause

The `timeout` flow operator in `Delay.kt:387-406` converts the upstream flow to a channel via `buffer(Channel.RENDEZVOUS).produceIn(this)` and then uses `whileSelect` with `onReceiveCatching` to read values. When the upstream channel is closed with an exception (as happens with `callbackFlow { close(IllegalStateException(...)) }`), the `onClosed` handler at line 396-397 was silently ignoring the exception and just returning `false` to exit the select loop:

```kotlin
.onClosed {
    return@onReceiveCatching false  // exception silently swallowed
}
```

## Fix

In `kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/flow/operators/Delay.kt:396-398`, the `onClosed` handler now re-throws any non-null exception before returning:

```kotlin
.onClosed {
    it?.let { throw it }
    return@onReceiveCatching false
}
```

This follows the established pattern used elsewhere in the codebase (e.g., `Channel.kt:337` and `Channel.kt:367` document `onClosed { if (it != null) throw it }` as the standard way to propagate exceptions from closed channels).

When the channel is closed normally (without an exception), the behavior is unchanged — it returns `false` to exit the select loop. When closed with an exception, the exception is now properly thrown and propagates to the downstream collector.
